narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated a 68% chance that you would fail the bar exam on your first try, based on patterns from thousands of past students. Your profile includes being in the bottom decile for grades in both your first and third years of school, an undergraduate GPA of 2.20, a notably low LSAT score of 24.50, coming from an upper-middle income family, identifying as a white male planning to study full-time. Compared to typical students—who average around the middle of their class in early and later years, a GPA over 3.20, and an LSAT near 37—your academic markers stand out as significantly lower, which heavily influenced the model's concern. The strongest factor pushing the prediction toward failure was your LSAT score, as it deviated far below the norm and signaled potential challenges in handling rigorous legal material. Next in influence came your first-year school decile, reflecting early struggles that the model sees as a red flag for long-term success on the bar. Your third-year decile ranking followed closely, reinforcing that pattern of underperformance relative to peers. Your family's upper-middle income level also nudged the prediction toward failure, perhaps because the model associates it with certain preparation gaps seen in similar cases. Your undergraduate GPA played a supporting role in this direction, being well below average and highlighting consistent academic hurdles. Factors like your race, gender, and full-time study plans had much smaller impacts and still leaned toward predicting failure, though they pale compared to your test scores and grades. Overall, no features pulled strongly in favor of passing, creating a one-sided risk profile. To boost your future bar exam odds, focus on rebuilding your academic foundation—consider targeted test prep to retake the LSAT if possible, seek tutoring to improve study habits shown in your low deciles, and build a stronger GPA through additional coursework or a post-bac program. With dedicated effort on these changeable areas, you could shift your profile closer to successful students and reapply stronger next time. We're rooting for you to turn this around.
